skill.name: context-degradation
skill.description: Diagnoses long-context failure modes in AI agents, including lost-in-middle behavior, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash.
skill.summary: Identify and reduce context degradation in AI agent workflows.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from markdown fences, ordinary prose, and illustrative examples. No prompt injection, network access, shell execution, secret access, or malicious behavior was found. The skill includes a Python helper script, so it carries low operational risk if users choose to run it.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown code fences and YAML or markdown examples, not executable shell commands. No subprocess, os.system, eval, or command interpolation behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are fenced documentation examples, not runtime code paths. Targeted review found no shell execution API usage in the Python script or skill instructions.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptography hits occur in prose about context degradation or generic sample logic, not calls to MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms. No evidence found of cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no imports or calls for cryptographic hashing. The matches are caused by unrelated words and documentation snippets.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Findings Are Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged text discusses preserving system prompts or uses a hallucination marker phrase. It does not inspect host information, enumerate users, read environment variables, or collect system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is natural-language guidance and a string list used for text analysis. No host reconnaissance APIs or filesystem enumeration are present.

low_findings.title: Bundled Python Analysis Script
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for script presence only. The skill includes a Python helper that analyzes supplied context with numpy and regular expressions. It does not perform network calls, shell execution, secret access, or file operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script is clearly present and executable as helper code, but its reviewed behavior is local analysis and console output only. The residual risk is dependency and runtime use, not malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Long AI conversations and agent runs can fail when important context is buried, polluted, or contradictory. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code identify degradation patterns and choose practical mitigation steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains lost-in-middle, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash patterns.
Provides symptoms to watch for when agent performance declines in long sessions.
Recommends mitigation strategies such as compaction, masking, partitioning, and isolation.
Includes reference examples for context health monitoring and recovery procedures.
Provides a Python helper for local context health analysis using heuristic scoring.
limitations: The included detector uses heuristics and simulated attention, not real model internals.
Model threshold examples may become outdated as model releases change.
It does not automatically repair an agent workflow without user-provided context.
It does not replace task-specific evaluation data or production observability.

use_cases.title: Debug Long Agent Runs
use_cases.description: Identify whether degraded answers come from buried requirements, accumulated errors, or conflicting context.

use_cases.title: Design Context Controls
use_cases.description: Choose compaction, filtering, retrieval, or isolation strategies before an agent reaches failure-prone context sizes.

use_cases.title: Review Support Failures
use_cases.description: Analyze transcripts where an assistant followed stale, irrelevant, or contradictory information.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Symptoms
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner diagnosis of a failing conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent transcript for context degradation symptoms. Identify lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, confusion, or clash. Then list the most likely cause.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Critical Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this prompt and context package. Tell me which information should move to the beginning or end, which information can be summarized, and which information should be removed.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Mitigation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan a durable workflow for long sessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a context management plan for this agent workflow. Include compaction triggers, retrieval rules, isolation boundaries, and validation checks for stale or conflicting context.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a context degradation postmortem for this failure case. Map evidence to degradation patterns, separate confirmed facts from hypotheses, and propose monitoring metrics.

output_examples.input: An agent forgot a key requirement from the middle of a long ticket history.
output_examples.output: The likely pattern is lost-in-middle degradation. Move the requirement to a current-task section, repeat it near the final instruction, and add a check before tool use.

output_examples.input: A coding assistant keeps using an outdated API after being corrected twice.
output_examples.output: This suggests context poisoning or clash. Remove the outdated reference, mark the corrected API as authoritative, and restart from verified context if errors persist.

output_examples.input: A support bot mixes billing rules with onboarding instructions.
output_examples.output: This indicates context confusion. Separate task contexts, load only the active policy set, and add routing checks before response generation.

best_practices: Keep current goals, constraints, and authority rules near attention-favored positions.
Filter retrieved material before adding it to active context.
Use explicit version and source priority rules when context contains similar documents.
anti_patterns: Do not keep every tool output in the active context by default.
Do not rely on a larger context window as the only mitigation.
Do not mix unrelated tasks in one long session without clear boundaries.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps diagnose failures caused by long or messy context in AI agent workflows.

faq.question: Does it run automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and an optional local Python helper for heuristic analysis.

faq.question: Can it measure real model attention?
faq.answer: No. The included script simulates attention patterns and should not be treated as model telemetry.

faq.question: Is it useful for coding agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps debug cases where coding agents lose requirements, follow stale context, or mix tasks.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access in the reviewed files.

faq.question: How should teams use it in production?
faq.answer: Use it as a diagnostic framework, then validate findings with logs, evaluations, and task-specific metrics.
